sentences.text: I'm born in Tuczyn till they threw us out.
Is this OK?
[INTERPOSING VOICES]
About Florida?
I come here to enjoy it.
Wherever I can, I want to grab [INAUDIBLE].
That's OK?
I am Adam--
I am Adam Schwartz.
In Tuczyn, 1912.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
OK.
Yes.
Poland.
My parents, I come from a big family.
And I am a tailor.
And I work in Tuczyn.
But the main [INAUDIBLE] folks, I work there till the war broke up.
A small, small, small town, very small.
Maybe under 20 families, maybe.
Maybe, yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Oh, yes, ma'am.
Yes.
Yes.
Yes.
No, ma'am.
No.
No.
No.
No.
In the beginning, when the Germans walked in with motorcycles, we clapped the hand.
You know, we were so happy, you know, with the, with the neighbors, you know, Gentile neighbors.
We was very happy they walked in.
No.
No.
No.
He sell cows, you know.
Yeah.
No, she was a housewife.
Yeah.
Housewife.
And a large-- you said a large family?
How many brothers and sisters did you have?
I have 4 brothers and five sisters.
No, not at all.
Not at all.
Later, when I was standing in a line, and a guy, which I used to go in school, in school with him, he had a [? swastika ?] on the left hand.
And I was surprised.
And I was stay in the line, because they give-- Gentile was stay in for bread, and Jews for some bread.
And he say, oh, he's a Jew.
And the guy took me out.
And he said, you circumcised?
And I stay for 25 minutes, and I was down, not, I don't know what to answer.
And I say, yes.
Now, he took me out.
He give me a couple over my butt.
I don't know what.
And he let me go, run home.
And I run home.
And mother start to cry.
And he say, don't you do this.
I want a better [? die ?] [? with ?] you doing this to go for bread.
And she was crying.
She break my heart.
This was this, this was the experience the first time.
Then, I saw we in trouble.
Yeah.
Right away, they suffered.
And they did right away in the street.
Right away, they used to call, oh, he is called a Jew because he had a beard.
And it didn't take long.
They cut off the beards.
Some young guys with a motorcycle come from the back, too, and say, Mr.
Schwartz, stay right there.
And they put him through a alley.
And they cut them, the beard.
Next day, Daddy cry.
And he was ashamed, because they left [? the ?] [? top ?] of it.
And he put a towel-- I remember like now-- around not to show what they did.
He was so, are we in deep, deep trouble?
So this the way that the war started for you and your family?
Started for my, for my parents.
Yeah.
No.
At this point, no.
Because only thing was a warning.
Jews have to go in the middle in the street, not on the sidewalk.
Yeah.
Yeah.
This was, yeah.
No, there was already--
It was already-- everything was disconnected [? the right. ?] OK.
So work, what were you doing with yourself in-- We was-- we have milk.
We have plenty, you know, from the cows.
And didn't take too long and they say-- the German say, they go [SPEAKING GERMAN].
A [SPEAKING GERMAN] is like, like different.
You know, this section is different, like two different park, you know, two different governors.
This belong to the Germans.
This supposed to be for the Poles.
Oh, but it was not right.
It was just a bluff.
And one night, they come in with a tube, you know?
And they say, [SPEAKING GERMAN[].
We start to cry.
And so you were forced to leave your home at that point?
We have to get out.
Not even an address, no.
Some of my sisters used to be with [INAUDIBLE] around the [? town. ?] And we stay outside.
And right away was a couple wagons [INAUDIBLE] was trucks.
Not trucks at this time.
We even didn't have trucks.
No, from the farmers, you know, some.
And they say, up on the wagon, you know, house by house.
And right away, the Gestapo go in the back and looked that there was somebody.
And who is, who left was shot.
And I say to my father, I don't go this way.
What I wanted to go in the [INAUDIBLE].
I want to go this way.
Maybe 10 miles away, my-- she was married by the name Hamprel.
He was a tailor there.
And I say, let's take-- we can [? take moving ?] one cow, that I help with father.
We go maybe 12 miles.
And we put it in, because start to get daytime.
It was [INAUDIBLE].
And we said-- and by his name was [INAUDIBLE].
And we put the cow there.
We say, we knock the window.
Will you let us in?
He say, give me the cow.
He put it in the barn.
And he say, OK, you come some other time.
I met my parent, met my father.
And my mother, with the other children, go to Belchatow.
In this time, was no ghetto.
It was still like Tuczyn, a little town.
The mother go this way, and I go this way with my father.
Then, he took the cow in.
And we go to-- and I knocked the door by my sister.
She start to cry and everybody.
She said, oh, I hope they don't come to me doing the same thing.
And we didn't know, unexpectedly.
And my sister's husband say, Adam, come down when get dark.
We go.
You take your father and go for the cow.
And we go put it in [? to ?] my sister.
We come.
He opened the door.
Right away, I saw, I saw how fast they go in the back.
And he say, run away.
Because the German go catch you.
Run away.
I say to father, let's go.
Please leave the cow.
Let's go.
We go back.
And he say, look, I don't can ruin your life to my sister's husband.
I go into Belchatow, to the ghetto.
It was no at this time ghetto.
I go to Belchatow.
And over there, they used to say that we're making factories, you know, and this.
And that's what they did in the beginning, for pens, for the blouses, for everything.
And we-- and he go to the-- he united with my mother.
And I was staying with my sister, with my brother-in-law.
Meantime, was a time, was the Polacks have to move.
And from five houses, they make one house.
And this was maybe 10 miles away from where I was with my brother-in-law.
They moved out the Polacks.
And they come in [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] Germans.
They said [SPEAKING GERMAN].
But Russian, [SPEAKING RUSSIAN]. you know, all the time, [? cities. ?]
But the name was the German.
I [? can't say this, ?] because the German was so nice.
And he said, [SPEAKING GERMAN] In German-- he talked German.
You, you stay here.
I take you in my house because I want for my wife a couple dresses, for my boy.
And one boy was in the army already.
You know, Hitler took them.
And he say, OK, you wait here.
I go take-- [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH].
How you say [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH]? .
[SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] is like, you give me the power.
I can take you, you know.
And he took us names.
And he give us to food so much.
Oh, yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
But he did that illegal.
He go, he take, he take, he take, how you say, a license, like a license.
I can take you out.
He took us, and we have so much food.
We bring home.
And he, all the time, give a ride.
And he no supposed to doing this.
Oh, but he give a ride.
And we can take home sugar and bread and give the sister, because she had two beautiful boys.
I never can forget.
And this German sent in to the sister because when they [INAUDIBLE] to come, they took out like a colony.
You know, took out.
He said, they're not German.
Why did they [? teach ?] the two boys?
Do you understand?
It was--
And I was thinking they haven't be forever.
You know, we, we eat.
I was like this.
Like this.
To his home.
He put us in second floor.
We had [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], you know, like a, a little factory.
Oh, yeah, my--
He was a champ.
My brother-in-law was a champ.
And he did it.
And I help him.
You know, I was a helper to him.
And I was thinking, never come [? back ?].
In the meantime, I take steps, because I didn't can forget the mother.
She is hungry there.
And I took steps.
I took a pound of sugar, two, two bread-- the big one, was the farmers make, big one.
And the guy tried to give me a ride to Belchatow, to give me a ride to give.
Oh, but he say, you have to drive the, the horses.
I don't want it.
If they catch you, if they catch you, you [? win. ?] You understand what he said to me?
I put a, a bundle.
I don't know.
But you know some in Europe, they have-- for trouble, they have over my face with everything.
I was bundled up.
I take the two horses.
I come to the Belchatow, to the ghetto.
They open up the opening, you know, a [? green-- ?] It's how you say, [INAUDIBLE], which they opened.
And you can go on in it.
And my father, I know where he live, was the name, [POLISH] And I [? lean. ?] I go so slow, [? methicodal, ?] Ho, ho, with the horses, ho.
[INAUDIBLE].
My mother, I can't forget.
My mother say, Adam is here.
I tore off the bundles on the sidewalk with the bread.
And I did [? not. ?] Goodbye, Mama.
And I go back to German.
I know now she have something to eat.
And I come home.
The brother-in-law say, you should not doing what you're doing.
And I say, I do everything for my parents.
I want to do everything for my parents.
We were-- we have a paradise with the German.
And he said, look, I can hold you just for a certain time here.
But I have to deliver you back in the ghetto, because I report that.
Meantime, we have so much to eat I think.
And it was better with the Germans.
The neighbors come in.
They say, you make me a skirt.
You make me this.
You make me-- what can be better?
And our teacher was to come  in to my sister's.
And then we said, [INAUDIBLE].
It was his name.
He say, never mind it.
Because my sister was worried.
Oh, stop it, [INAUDIBLE].
She was worried that the kids don't have education.
The German [INAUDIBLE] come in, and she teach the-- a old lady.
And she was to from the [INAUDIBLE], from the border, from the Russian border over there.
Her name was Abrams.
I remember like now.
And she say-- and one time, she come in, say Mr.
Hamprel, [SPEAKING GERMAN].
You have to move in here.
Because if not, they kill you.
Try to save your kids.
My sister took the [INAUDIBLE], everything, and she-- five miles away.
And she gives, it's a Polack.
She give the kids.
One was Bel and Aron.
She give the kids.
And the kids say, where you take me?
What-- Sit there till I come back.
I make this short.
Then, he start to deliver us back.
And he say, like, Monday, you will have to go back in the ghetto.
But I didn't realize what a ghetto.
And the sister start to leave everything and go.
At nighttime, she go over and say, this is my kids.
He take everything, save him.
And I was stand right there.
We kiss the kids, say, [?
Mama, ?] don't.
They go.
And we was delivered, with my brother-in-law of one wagon.
He was so nice, that German.
He delivered us till they opened the gates to go-- that gates, it's called.
He was there.
Every day, I saw a different picture.
What was the picture?
Oh, he was shot.
He was catch because he bought a chicken.
He was a bullet.
Then, there was one time where you pump water.
You know, where you pump water?
They took out beautiful girls with motorcycles, young punks, you know, maybe 16, 17 years.
And they tell them to take off their blouses.
And they was drunk, you see.
And when you're drunk, you don't can do nothing.
They have a satisfact-- take off the blouse around.
There was six beautiful girls, I remember.
Beautiful.
And it was so cold there.
And the girls [INAUDIBLE] He have a pipe like the police.
And he give it right over the head.
And she start to run so fast until they almost fall down.
And this was almost dark.
And they say, go home.
And I looked this through, through the top.
And I said, what, Daddy, what be next?
This time-- you remember, the time was a time to run to the Russians.
This was the time before closing.
And I said, Daddy, let's leave everything.
And we have a house, you know, a big house.
He say, I leave my property and to go away from everything?
What you doing?
[? god ?] [? is within? ?] [?
God is within. ?] I said, let's run away.
It's time.
If not, let's run the forest.
You know, this time you exist [INAUDIBLE].
You know [INAUDIBLE]?
And Father, said, oh, [INAUDIBLE].
Daddy, they pull your beard out.
Let's run away.
No.
Another go, month go by, another man stabbed to death.
[INAUDIBLE].
The youngster brother was in our Hebrew school, you know.
The way when he come home, they catch him off a wagon.
And the mama didn't see him anymore.
And he is still alive, the same one.

Anyway, making short this-- one time, they saying they're supposed to deliver 300 people [INAUDIBLE] And right away, with Jewish couples, you know, they [? pull it. ?] And then [? the religious, ?] they, they grab your brother.
Your brother, they looked off the list.
It's already the Gestapo come out from the house.
Hey, [SPEAKING GERMAN].
They [? caught ?] him, him.
It was 300.
And this come in a wagon.
They took it away.
Till now, nobody know it, you know.
The first one, no.
No.
The next week, they're saying they needed another 100.
Then they catch my little brother, the youngest, the youngest brother.
And Mama say, let's go run away.
It was too late.
Right away was [INAUDIBLE] on the streets.
We have to be delivered to the big ghetto.
This was Lodz.
I was locked up.
You know what mean locked up?
You didn't can go out in the street.
Here, was [INAUDIBLE].
The only thing was you can do is go up and kill yourself.
Then, the father stay like you, like-- and he say, [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] Now, I tell you in English how it was said.
I double cross you.
I start to cry.
He start to cry.
Too late.
Daddy, is too late.
They took out-- little by little, they took out the young people.
But the old people, OK, we have to go to Lodz.
Oh, yeah, was-- between time was factories, little factories.
And the same, they closed up.
Is common.
I remember the mothers say, OK.
She have a golden chain, you know, when she married.
And was a oven, was over there we, we lived.
And another Jew give us the apartment.
And she say, you see, I put the-- how do you say the--
The chain over here.
Who goes surviving can come back and pick it.
Not just this-- she took the rings over there and put it in.
And I say to myself, OK.
Where they put in.
This was one day.
The second and third day, I come in front, you see, because I know how to run upstairs to live.
And they come in, because the number have to be revealed.
When they say 300 Juden, that's it.
Now, I come close.
What I do when I run away?
Where I go run?
And I'll never forget in my whole life.
The third day, I see they put [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] You know what mean [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH]?
What to hang people, you know.
And they took out [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] 10 young guys like this.
And we all have to see it, how they hang them in Belchatow.
They hanged it up.
When the Gestapo put off the tube, everybody can go back at the house.
The next day, come again, maybe three or the four wagons.
And they put all of them after.
And we come to Lodz, a ghetto.
This was one [? tablet. ?] We come to Lodz, was the [?
Rimner ?] Street, the [?
Rimner ?] Street, and next, it is Ga-- anyway, you don't know.
And we come down from the wagons.
And Mother was right away [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH].
In the beginning, I didn't know what this means, [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH].
That was the police, the kapos from the Lodz ghetto, say, you don't see your mother, that she go run away away.
[SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH].
Because on the day, it was trains to Auschwitz, see?
But did you know that the trains were going to Auschwitz then?
No, I didn't.
I didn't know this.
But I know they kill, you see, because I saw what-they're doing.
Yeah.
Me, then they put mother off the wagon, [INAUDIBLE], and I go too off the, off the wagon.
Meantime, I see the Polack bring the both kids by the ears to deliver the two boys.
And they say, [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] I stretch on the-- was other people hold me.
And they put them off the next wagon.
And when we come to Lodz, I was thinking I can meet them there.
You know, I see they put with my mother with, with my sister, off another wagon.
And a couple told me, they're going of [NON-ENGLISH] I didn't know [NON-ENGLISH].
Later, [INAUDIBLE].
[INAUDIBLE] if you can take much.
Right away, I come a little farther.
But the, but the Gestapo stay [POLISH] Street.
I meet a guy from our town, from Tuczyn.
[INAUDIBLE] That camp, you come here?
You are so smart.
How come you do?
[INAUDIBLE] Yeah, I'm stupid.
I'm stupid.
But anyway, I do you a favor.
It's called [INAUDIBLE].
[SPEAKING POLISH] that sound [SPEAKING POLISH] it mean vegetable, you know.
And everybody get three pounds potatoes.
And he kissed me.
And he have a son, was I saw that he was shot.
And he say, he's living?
And I say.
And he was thinking, I was, I was with him son, you see, together.
And I lie.
He took me off this.
He saved my life.
He took me out.
I was alone.
Yeah, it was maybe 70 people.
Everybody get a room, empty room.
And the people was taken out to Auschwitz.
I didn't know what, what is going-- and he say, you stay there till tomorrow, nine o'clock.
I come pick you up.
He was there.
Separate--
Run away, they told me [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] Don't think they're going to [? live ?].
OK.
You know how I survive?
I go often, often [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH].
And I see what's going on.
So meantime, I took a couple potatoes in my sock.
I was the richest.
I was a millionaire.
And I go home.
Was across from the other when was this other [INAUDIBLE].
In another, there was sitting [? three ?] beautiful girls.
And she looked through the window.
She's like, what you doing?
I say, I , I work of [SPEAKING NON-ENGLISH] often, what to sell, what to give it, to the people food, you know, food.
Right away, she come over.
Darling, I have this [INAUDIBLE].
Please give me a potato.
Poor darlings.
[INAUDIBLE]
And I say, where's your mama?
Forget it, she said.
I don't see my mama.
We have the same [INAUDIBLE].
You know, and I have three rooms, beautiful rooms.
It was [INAUDIBLE] bras.
I saw dresses there on the floor.
And I go in bed, and I lay in bed.
And tomorrow, nine o'clock, I have to see-- [INAUDIBLE] was his name.
He dead now.
This guy saved my life.
Later, in a couple days later, I make this short, because I didn't want to tell you what I saw in the ghetto.
But anyway, I skip this.
We go [? close ?] in the vegetable, especially the [INAUDIBLE] will come up.
And he said, Adam, they go catching single people tonight.
Run away.
I stand up, and I opened the door.
I opened the--
